subject: Calculus Prerequisite
topic: §27 Graphing Systems of Linear Inequalities in Two Variables
question: Solve the linear system $-3x + 4y \le 12$, $x + 2y < 6$, $-x + 5y \ge -5$.
explanation: We graph the solution set to each linear inequality on the same set of axes. The overlapping region is the triangle with corners $(0, 3)$, $\left(-\frac{80}{11}, -27\right)$, $\left(\frac{40}{7}, 1\right)$ as shown in Figure 27.2 Figure 27.2
